description: Manifest of analyzed tournament replays. Feeds the AI overhaul Phase 8 opening book and ad-hoc archetype lookups (e.g. 'what's the canonical T2 line for X?').

replays.match_outcome: 1-1 with Game 3 truncated by transcript; Game 3 momentum favored Alexandre at end of caption window
replays.notes: First replay logged. Player full names not confirmed in Limitless top-255 standings — handles match caster pronunciation. Decklists are representative archetypal lists from the same tournament (Cipolla 8th, Tallone 106th).

replays.round: Swiss Round 13 (final round, win-and-in for Top 16)
replays.match_outcome: Netti wins 2-1 (G1 Lorenzo, G2 Lorenzo concession at 5min, G3 Netti via Unfair Stamp + Boss + Switch combo)
replays.notes: First documented case in our replay corpus of a player INTENTIONALLY conceding Game 2 for time-management reasons. Two NEW archetypes introduced: mega-starmie-froslass (distinct from mega-starmie-dusknoir) and cynthia-garchomp.

Player full names from Limitless top 16 standings. Mateusz is the eventual tournament champion. NEW archetype: grimmsnarl-froslass (Maman's-prefix support engine + Frostlass Freezing Shroud + Spike Muff Gym).

NEW archetype: crustle (Mysterious Rocking Inability EX-immunity + Mega Kangaskhan ex backup + Cornerstone Mask Ogerpon ex). 'Aggressive Dragapult' (3 Rare Candy, basic-EX bench) variant differs from Mateusz's Dudunsparce-engine variant — engine should split into multiple dragapult sub-archetypes.

replays.match_outcome: Elmar wins 2-1 (G1 Mega Kanga rapid-fire 200, G2 Elmar 'missed math', G3 Mega Kanga 3-heads again)
replays.notes: NEW archetype: mega-starmie-dusknoir (distinct from mega-starmie-froslass — uses Cursed Blast for Crustle-counter rather than Mega Frostlass damage scaling). Cursed Blast + Jetting Blow exact-OHKO combo demonstrated.

replays.match_outcome: Mateusz wins 2-1 (G1 Netti via Boss + Roserade ramp, G2 Mateusz prioritized Roserade KOs, G3 double-Munkidori Adrena-Brain finish)
replays.notes: Hero's Cape on non-EX Dudunsparce is called game-winning by casters. Validates 'protecting 1-prize attackers' as a high-value heuristic.

replays.match_outcome: Mateusz wins 2-0 (G1 Destructive Drill bypassed Mysterious Rocking, G2 Mind Bend Confusion lock with no Switch in Elmar's hand)
replays.notes: Mateusz is the 2026 Prague Regional Champion. Game-1 highlight: 3 of 4 Poké Pads PRIZED — Mateusz adapted via Brock's Scouting. Game-2 highlight: 16-CARD Erratic Machinations discard. Final blow: Munkidori Mind Bend over Dragon Headbutt (Confusion lock vs no Switch). Verify Erratic Machinations + Asher Roller's Mischief + Missed-Energy-Confusion-interaction in engine.

tournament_summary_prague_2026.champion: Mateusz Łaszkiewicz (Dragapult/Dudunsparce, Hero's Cape ACE SPEC)
tournament_summary_prague_2026.finalist: Elmar Tresp (Crustle, Mega Kangaskhan ex backup, Hero's Cape ACE SPEC)
tournament_summary_prague_2026.top_8_archetype_breakdown: Dragapult ×3, Crustle ×3, Mega Starmie ×1, Cynthia's Garchomp ×1, Raging Bolt ×1, Ogerpon/Meganium ×1 (10 total accounting for top-cut bracket spots)
tournament_summary_prague_2026.key_format_takeaway: Crustle is the key disruption deck — if your deck has no anti-EX-immunity tech (Cursed Blast / Tenacious Tail / Destructive Drill / non-EX attackers / Curse Blast), Crustle is structurally near-unwinnable.
tournament_summary_prague_2026.ace_spec_meta: Hero's Cape ascendant — both finalists ran it. Unfair Stamp also viable. Mega Kangaskhan ex's 'rapid-fire combo' (coin-flip per energy) showed extreme variance impact.
